// scenarios: Short seed ideas for the Planner. The bot picks a random subset (size = scenarioSampleSize) each iteration and asks the Planner to choose or blend one into a concrete plan. Keep each line a single sentence that a normal Windows user could plausibly do in a few steps.
scenarios: Open the Start menu and browse the pinned apps.
Open Notepad and jot down a short reminder.
Open Calculator and add a few numbers.
Check the current time and date from the system tray.
Open File Explorer and look at the Documents folder.
Open File Explorer and look at the Pictures folder.
Open File Explorer and navigate to This PC.
Open Settings and look at the System page.
Open Settings and check the display configuration.
Open Settings and look at the sound mixer.
Open Settings and review the notifications page.
Open Settings and check the Windows Update page (do not install updates).
Open Settings and view the installed apps list.
Open Task Manager and look at running processes, then close it.
Open the Snipping Tool and dismiss it without capturing anything.
Open Paint and draw a quick scribble, then close without saving.
Open the on-screen keyboard from accessibility tools and close it.
Open the Magnifier briefly and close it.
Open the default web browser and visit a news website.
Open the default web browser and search for the weather.
Open the default web browser and look up a Wikipedia article.
Open the default web browser and visit a popular search engine.
Open the default web browser and read a short documentation page.
Open the default web browser and check the front page of GitHub.
Open the default web browser and visit Stack Overflow.
Open the default web browser and watch the front page of a video site.
Open a new tab in the default web browser and switch between tabs.
Open the default web browser, scroll a long article, and scroll back to the top.
Open the default web browser and use Ctrl+F to find a word on a page.
Open the default web browser and zoom in with Ctrl+Plus, then reset with Ctrl+0.
Open the default web browser, open bookmarks, and close the bookmarks panel.
Open the default web browser's history view and close it.
Open the default web browser's downloads view and close it.
Right-click on the desktop and dismiss the context menu.
Right-click in File Explorer's blank area and dismiss the menu.
Press Win+D to show the desktop, then bring back the previous window with Alt+Tab.
Use Alt+Tab to cycle through open windows.
Use Win+Tab to open Task View and dismiss it.
Snap a window to the left half of the screen with Win+Left.
Snap a window to the right half of the screen with Win+Right.
Maximise the current window with Win+Up, then restore it with Win+Down.
Minimise the foreground window with Win+Down.
Open the Action Center / notifications panel with Win+N or Win+A and close it.
Open the volume flyout from the system tray and close it.
Open the network flyout from the system tray and close it.
Adjust the system volume with the keyboard media keys.
Mute and then unmute the system volume.
Open the clipboard history with Win+V (if enabled) and close it.
Open the emoji panel with Win+. and close it.
Open the Run dialog with Win+R and dismiss it without running anything.
Open the Run dialog with Win+R, type 'notepad', press Enter, then close Notepad.
Open File Explorer with Win+E and close it.
Open the system Search bar with Win+S, type a harmless query, and dismiss the popup.
Open the Start menu, type the name of an installed app, dismiss the menu without launching anything.
Open the Photos app and browse to the first item, then close it.
Open the Weather app if installed and close it.
Open the Clock app and look at the timers tab.
Open the Game Bar with Win+G and close it.
Open the Windows Security app and view the status page, then close it.
Open Control Panel and dismiss it.
Open the Device Manager (read-only browse), then close it.
Open the Services management console (read-only), then close it.
Open Disk Cleanup, cancel the dialog without running it.
Open the Resource Monitor briefly and close it.
Open PowerShell, type a harmless command like 'Get-Date', and close the window.
Open Command Prompt, type 'dir', and close the window.
Open Windows Terminal if installed, switch tabs, and close it.
Open a Notepad document, type a few lines, undo with Ctrl+Z, redo with Ctrl+Y, close without saving.
Open Notepad, change the font from the Settings (gear) menu, and close without saving.
Open Notepad, toggle word wrap from the View menu, and close without saving.
Open the Character Map utility if available, browse a few glyphs, and close it.
Switch the keyboard language with Win+Space and switch it back.
Open the touch keyboard if available and close it.
Use the magnifier shortcut Win+Plus once to zoom in, then Win+Esc to close.
Open the high-contrast settings page (just view, do not change anything).
Hover over the system tray clock and read the tooltip.
Click the show-desktop sliver in the bottom-right corner, then bring windows back.
Open the Recycle Bin from File Explorer and close it without deleting anything.
Open File Explorer, change the view to Details, then back to Large Icons.
Open File Explorer, sort the current folder by Name, then by Date Modified.
Open File Explorer, toggle the preview pane, then toggle it off.
Open File Explorer, open Quick Access, and browse a folder.
Open File Explorer and use Ctrl+L to focus the address bar.
Open a text file from the desktop (if any) with Notepad, then close it.
Open the Print Queue from Settings, observe it is empty, and close it.
Open the Bluetooth settings page (just view), then close it.
Open the Wi-Fi settings page (just view), then close it.
Open the Mouse settings page and view the pointer options, then close it.
Open the Keyboard settings page (just view), then close it.
Open the Personalization settings and look at the Background page, then close it.
Open the Themes settings page (just view), then close it.
Open the Lock screen settings page (just view), then close it.
Open the Time & Language settings (just view), then close it.
Open the About page in Settings and read the device information.
Open the Storage settings page and view the disk usage, then close it.
Open the Power & Battery settings page and view the current plan, then close it.
Open the Default Apps settings page and scroll through the list, then close it.
Open the Apps & Features list, scroll through it, and close it without changing anything.
Open the Default Browser settings page (just view), then close it.
Tidy any stray dialogs by pressing Escape a few times and showing the desktop with Win+D.
// scenarioSampleSize: How many scenarios are shown to the Planner each iteration. Clamped 1..10.
// constraints: Hard safety rails enforced by the controller BEFORE anything reaches the dongle. The validator should reject dangerous plans; this is the second line of defence.
// planner: The Planner picks a goal from the scenarios and proposes a small set of steps. Leave systemPrompt empty to use the built-in default.
// validator: The Validator inspects the proposed plan and rejects anything dangerous (deletions, shutdown, lock, breaking the bot itself).
// executor: The Executor turns each plan step into HID actions one screenshot at a time.
